json_data: Full stack development involves front-end (user interface), back-end (server-side logic), and database management.
Front-end technologies include HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue.js, and Angular.
Back-end technologies include Node.js, Python, Ruby, and Java.
Common databases include MySQL, MongoDB, and PostgreSQL.
DevOps tools include Git, Docker, and Jenkins.
8-13% annual job growth projected for full stack developers through 2024.
There are currently 64,957 job openings for full stack developers in the United States.
Average salaries range from $85,000 (entry-level) to $284,000 (senior-level).
The MEAN stack consists of MongoDB, Express.js, Angular, and Node.js.
The MERN stack consists of MongoDB, Express.js, React, and Node.js.
The LAMP stack consists of Linux, Apache, MySQL, and PHP.
JAMstack is a modern approach using JavaScript, APIs, and Markup.
JavaScript has a 68% adoption rate among developers according to Stack Overflow's 2023 survey.
93% of developers use Git for version control according to a JetBrains 2023 survey.
72% of back-end development projects adopted JavaScript-powered technologies like Node.js in 2023.
The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics predicts 22% growth in full stack developer jobs from 2020 to 2030.
Entry-level full stack developers earn $85,000-$107,000 annually.
Mid-level full stack developers earn $115,555 annually on average.
Senior-level full stack developers earn $161,000-$284,000 annually.
Full stack developer salaries average $190,333 in San Francisco.
Full stack developer salaries average $173,000 in New York City.
Full stack developer salaries average $150,000 in Austin.
JavaScript is the most used programming language among developers.
Full stack developers should master version control, especially Git.
The recommended learning path starts with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript basics.
With full-time dedication, one can become a full stack developer in as little as 90 days.
Full stack development offers career progression from Junior Developer to Technical Lead, Architect, or CTO.
